prompt: A Gupta Empire courtyard scene showing men and women creating a large Rangoli pattern on a smooth stone floor. The center is a small simple geometric design expanding outward into overwhelming complexity with elaborate spirals, symmetrical motifs, dense geometric forms. Vibrant colored powders, stone carved pillars, terracotta lamps, classical Indian attire. No text or symbols.
tradition_approach: The Gupta-era Rangoli style uses radially symmetric geometric patterns made from colored powders, starting with a simple motif at the center and progressively adding more intricate dense elaborate designs toward the outer edges to represent exponential growth in complexity.
